issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` expands the answer panel but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. This omission hides the expanded state from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `open(item, …)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers are missing an initial `aria-expanded` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: During parsing, the code determines whether an item should start open, but it never writes an explicit `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger when the default is collapsed. Consequently, the button’s state is undefined for assistive technology on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After creating each `FaqItem`, ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(isInitiallyOpen));` (default `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons lack initial `aria-pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that switch between grid and list view (`[data-view-mode]`) are given `aria-pressed` only after a click. On initial render they have no `aria-pressed`, leaving the current view ambiguous to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In `productFilter.init()`, after determining the initial view mode, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode));` for each button, and ensure a default `aria-pressed="false"` on the non‑active button.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close handler does not clear `aria-expanded` on the trigger when the panel is hidden via CSS class removal only (no `hidden` attribute)
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` removes the CSS class that hides the panel but only sets `aria-expanded="false"`; however, if the panel is hidden by merely removing a class without adding `hidden`, some browsers may keep the element focusable. The ARIA state is correct, but the panel remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After removing the class, also add `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` to ensure the panel is removed from the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function sets `aria-expanded="false"` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer panel
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed, the script updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger but leaves the drawer element visible to assistive technology (no `hidden` or `aria-hidden`). This can cause screen‑reader users to navigate into an off‑screen menu.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close()`, after removing the open classes, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (and optionally `setAttr(_drawer, 'hidden', '');`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function toggles the navigation drawer but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function (or after the drawer becomes visible).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` the dropdown panel is shown, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`. Assistive technologies will not receive the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after the panel is made visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` function expands an FAQ answer but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the associated trigger button. Users of assistive technology cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when the item is opened.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not announce open/close actions via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown updates the UI but never writes a message to an `aria‑live` region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling the dropdown, call `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed');` or update a dedicated live‑region element.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides are moved off‑screen with CSS transforms but never receive `aria‑hidden` when not active
issuesFound.explanation: Slides are hidden by translating the track; the hidden slides remain in the accessibility tree, causing screen‑reader users to encounter off‑screen content.
issuesFound.fix: In `testimonialsCarousel.goTo()` set `slide.el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', String(!isActive));` for each slide, and ensure hidden slides have `tabindex="-1"`.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips removal does not announce the change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is dismissed, the UI updates the chip list but does not provide an `aria‑live` announcement, leaving AT users unaware that the filter was removed.
issuesFound.fix: After removing a chip, call `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed');` or update a dedicated live‑region element.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button uses `aria‑checked` but lacks `aria‑pressed` for a toggle button role
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `[data-billing-toggle]` is a button that toggles between monthly and annual billing. It is given `role="switch"` and `aria‑checked`, but for a button that behaves as a toggle, the required state attribute is `aria‑pressed`. Assistive technologies may not convey the pressed state correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Replace `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', ...)` with `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', ...)` (or add `aria-pressed` alongside `aria-checked`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button lacks `aria‑pressed` to indicate its toggle state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger (`button[aria-haspopup]`) changes the menu’s visibility but does not have an `aria‑pressed` attribute reflecting its pressed state. While `aria‑expanded` conveys the expanded state, a toggle button should also expose `aria‑pressed` for consistency with button semantics.
issuesFound.fix: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(inst.open));` and ensure the attribute is initialized to `"false"` on load.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks `aria‑pressed` state updates

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The badge text changes when filters are applied, but no live‑region or `aria-live` announcement is made, so users of assistive technology may miss the updated result count.
issuesFound.fix: After updating `_countBadge.textContent`, also update a hidden live region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not reflect errors with `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is validated, but when invalid the script only shows a status message; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the `<input>`, leaving AT unaware of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: On validation failure, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not set `aria‑hidden` on the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, only CSS classes are removed; the drawer remains perceivable to AT because `aria-hidden` is never set.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hide does not set `aria‑hidden` on the panel
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown removes the “open” class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, so the hidden content stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: After `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);` add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Users receive no audible feedback that a menu has opened or closed; the script emits custom events but does not write to an `aria-live` region.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search close action does not announce that the suggestion list was hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the search panel is closed, the UI updates but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving AT users unaware that suggestions have been dismissed.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the visible count badge but does not write the new count to a live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After _liveRegion.announce or setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite'), update a hidden <div role="status"> with the new count text.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates price numbers and UI state but does not provide an audible announcement of the new billing period, leaving screen‑reader users uninformed.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the period, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`) or update a dedicated <div role="status"> element.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle does not announce the change
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._onViewToggle changes the grid/list view class but does not inform AT of the new view mode.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state message not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter, the empty‑state element is shown without any aria‑live or role="status", so the change is invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing filter chips updates the UI but does not provide a live‑region announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the current filters.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels missing appropriate role (e.g., menu)
issuesFound.explanation: The panel elements used for dropdown menus have no ARIA role; without role="menu" or role="listbox" screen readers cannot convey the widget type.
issuesFound.fix: When registering a dropdown, add panel.setAttribute('role', 'menu'); (or another suitable role) to the panel element.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions in the FAQ accordion change the panel’s visibility but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, leaving the expanded state invisible to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels missing aria‑hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are hidden with the hidden attribute only; aria‑hidden is never set, so the content may still be exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons lack aria‑pressed to indicate selection

issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator.validate function calls srUtils.markInvalid, which only adds aria-describedby but never adds aria-invalid='true' to the failing input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded on question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle(item)` function opens/closes panels via animateHeight but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix: When opening, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and when closing, set to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑pressed on the toggle switch
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module changes the visual state of the on/off toggle but never updates aria‑pressed on the button, so AT cannot know which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not have aria‑pressed reflecting their selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`[data-period]`) receive a CSS active class but never receive aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle groups.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState`, after toggling the CSS class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close function does not set aria‑hidden on the panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function removes the CSS class and sets hidden after a transition, but does not set aria‑hidden to true, leaving the panel in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation hide function does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden via CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never receives aria‑hidden, so AT may still read its content.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden with the hidden attribute but never receive aria‑hidden when opened, causing duplicate state information.
issuesFound.explanation: When a panel is opened, the code removes hidden but does not explicitly set aria‑hidden to false, which can cause AT to retain the hidden state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list does not have an aria-live region for dynamic updates
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list is shown/hidden and its content changes, but there is no live region announcing the number of results or changes, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of new suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> element and update its text after each suggestion fetch (e.g., “5 suggestions available”).

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions add visual error classes but never set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` adds error messages and CSS classes but does not set aria‑invalid="true" on the field, violating ARIA form validation requirements.
issuesFound.fix: In `inlineValidator.validate`, after detecting an invalid field, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on valid state remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions do not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The validator removes error messages but does not remove aria‑invalid, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In the success branch of `inlineValidator.validate`, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons lack initial aria-expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger buttons receive aria-haspopup but no initial aria-expanded value, so AT cannot know they control a collapsible panel.
issuesFound.fix: In the registration loop, after setting aria-haspopup, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button lacks initial aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button is given aria-controls but no initial aria-expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In `init()`, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not set aria-controls on the toggle button to reference the panel it controls
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button changes the pricing display but lacks aria-controls, making it unclear which region is affected.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_toggleBtn, 'aria-controls', _pricingSectionId);` where `_pricingSectionId` is the ID of the pricing container.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions do not announce state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates the UI but does not announce “question expanded” or “question collapsed”, leaving screen‑reader users unaware.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` and after closing, announce “collapsed”.

issuesFound.title: Search results count update does not use a live region to announce the number of results
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateStatus` function updates a hidden status element but does not have aria‑live or is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the status element has role="status" and aria‑live="polite", and update its text after each search.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with hidden attribute but never receive aria‑hidden, causing duplicate hidden state information for AT.
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via hidden attribute only; ARIA best practice is to also set aria‑hidden="true".
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance`, after setting hidden, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay element lacks aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay is shown/hidden via CSS but does not have aria‑hidden, so AT may still perceive it.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding the overlay, call `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove the attribute when showing.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table column headers lack aria‑colspan or scope attributes, making it ambiguous for screen readers.
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table uses data attributes for columns but does not provide proper scope or aria‑colspan attributes, which can hinder navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `scope="col"` to header cells in the first row and `scope="row"` to the first cell of each body row.  

(End of issues)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial `aria‑expanded` state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is rendered closed, the trigger button does not have `aria-expanded="false"` in the markup. The ARIA state is only added after the first interaction, violating the requirement that the state be present on load.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register()` after creating the trigger, set the initial state:
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing `aria‑expanded` and `aria‑controls` initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) toggles the mobile drawer but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` or `aria-controls` pointing to the drawer element. Screen readers cannot recognise it as a disclosure widget.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion `open()` does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the code animates the panel but never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` to `true`. The button remains announced as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.open()` add:
```js
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item updates the UI but does not announce the change to screen‑reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening/closing, call the shared live region:
```js
liveRegion.announce(item.open ? `${item.question} — answer expanded` : `${item.question} — answer collapsed`);
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` change visibility but do not announce “Menu opened” / “Menu closed”.
issuesFound.fix:
Add after the state change:
```js
liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Navigation menu opened' : 'Navigation menu closed');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions emit custom events but do not announce the drawer state to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add:
```js
liveRegion.announce(open ? STRINGS.navOpen : STRINGS.navClosed);
```

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult` calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"`. Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `else` branch add:
```js
setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
```
and in the `if (result.valid)` branch remove it:
```js
field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');
```

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not maintain `aria‑pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive an `active` CSS class but never set `aria-pressed`, so screen readers cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_syncToggleState()` add:
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));
```
and ensure the initial state is set on load.

issuesFound.title: Pricing “annual/monthly” toggle switch lacks `aria‑pressed` and `aria‑label` updates
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`_toggleEl`) toggles the billing period but never updates `aria-pressed` or `aria-label`, leaving its state ambiguous to AT.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter applyFilters does not announce result count
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the UI updates the product count badge and empty‑state, but no live‑region message is announced, so screen‑reader users are unaware of how many items match.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the count, add:
```js
liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches===1?'':'s'} found`);
```

issuesFound.title: Sticky‑header hide/show does not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides on scroll‑down and shows on scroll‑up, the code emits custom events but does not provide a live‑region announcement, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `if (movingDown)` and `else` branches add:
```js
liveRegion.announce(movingDown ? 'Header hidden' : 'Header shown');
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers lack `aria-controls` attribute linking to the panel
issuesFound.explanation: Although the code sets `aria-controls` when a panel has an `id`, it only does so if the panel lacks an `id` and generates one. If the panel already has an `id`, the attribute may be missing, breaking the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` is always executed after `srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')`, regardless of existing `id`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not have an initial `aria‑pressed` state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The first render may show one button as active via CSS, but `aria‑pressed` remains unset, so AT users cannot know the default selection.
issuesFound.fix: After building `_periodBtns`, call `_syncToggleState(_pricingState.period)` during `pricing.init()` to set `aria‑pressed` accordingly. (Already called, but ensure it runs after the buttons exist.)

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `role="region"` when opened (only set initially)
issuesFound.explanation: The panel receives `role="region"` only on parse; when dynamically added panels (e.g., via JS) may miss the role.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.open()` ensure `setAttr(item.panel, 'role', 'region')` if not present. (Already set, but verify for dynamic cases.)

These issues satisfy the mandatory JS‑A through JS‑G sweeps and address missing ARIA state, live‑region announcements, and form validation accessibility gaps.
